Migrate to Vue 3, Vite, and Tailwind 4

- Vue 2 -> Vue 3: use createApp, built-in composition API (remove @vue/composition-api)
- vue-cli/webpack -> Vite: add vite.config.ts, replace vue.config.js and babel.config.js
- Tailwind 1 -> Tailwind 4: use @tailwindcss/vite, update CSS directives
- GitHub Actions: Node 20 -> 24, remove NODE_OPTIONS flag
- Remove: core-js, @vue/composition-api, vue-template-compiler, babel, purgecss, vti, vue-cli
- Add: vue-tsc, eslint 8 with Vue 3 rules, @tailwindcss/vite

commit.message:
docs: add CLAUDE.md hierarchy via init-claude skill

Generated by running /init-claude on this repository.

Root CLAUDE.md (12 lines, always-loaded):
- One-sentence description
- Two-sentence structure explanation differentiating the two skill sets

plugins/agents-initializer/CLAUDE.md (14 lines, on-demand):
- Plugin spec conventions for skills/ and agents/ subdirectories
- marketplace.json source path constraint
- Subagent limitations

.claude/rules/ (path-scoped, on-demand only):
- agent-files.md: triggered on plugins/agents-initializer/agents/*.md
  YAML frontmatter spec, model/tools/maxTurns constraints
- plugin-skills.md: triggered on plugins/agents-initializer/skills/*/SKILL.md
  agent delegation requirement, no inline analysis
- standalone-skills.md: triggered on skills/*/SKILL.md
  self-contained requirement, no agent references

Always-loaded: 12 lines. On-demand: 47 lines across 4 files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: project-agents-initializer
repository.description: Project AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md initializer based on papers studies for optimum file definition plugin.

commit.message:
refactor: clean install path separation — plugin vs npx skills add

Two distinct skill sets now serve two distinct installation paths:

plugins/agents-initializer/skills/ — Claude Code plugin (agent-delegating)
  Skills delegate analysis to proper Claude Code subagents:
  - codebase-analyzer, scope-detector, file-evaluator
  - Keeps orchestrator context clean via isolated subagent execution
  - Follows official Claude Code plugin spec
  - Installed via /plugin install agents-initializer@project-agents-initializer

skills/ (repo root) — npx skills add (standalone)
  Skills perform all analysis inline with direct bash/file commands
  - No subagent delegation, no Task tool, no agent dependency
  - Compatible with any AI coding tool (Copilot, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.)
  - npx recursive search finds root skills/ last, taking precedence

Also removes agents/ copies that were incorrectly added inside each plugin
skill directory — those polluted the Claude Code plugin context by introducing
non-standard .md files inside the skills/ structure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: bundle agent files inside each skill folder for npx skills add

npx skills add copies the full skill directory (SKILL.md + all sibling
files/folders). Previously, agents lived only in plugins/agents-initializer/agents/
which is picked up by Claude Code's plugin installer but NOT by npx skills add.

This commit copies all three agent definitions into each skill's own agents/
subdirectory so both installation methods provide complete agent files:

- Claude Code plugin install: agents from plugins/agents-initializer/agents/
  registered as agents-initializer:* namespace
- npx skills add: agents copied to ~/.copilot/skills/<skill-name>/agents/
  available as inline reference for each skill

Authoritative definitions remain in plugins/agents-initializer/agents/.
Skill-level copies are for npx skills add compatibility only.

Also updates README to document this structure and explain the install
behavior for each method.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: project-agents-initializer
repository.description: Project AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md initializer based on papers studies for optimum file definition plugin.

commit.message:
fix: restructure to satisfy both Claude Code plugin and npx skills install

Claude Code plugin system requires source to point to a subdirectory
(not root '.'), so plugin files move to plugins/agents-initializer/.
npx skills add does a recursive SKILL.md search, so it finds skills
in plugins/agents-initializer/skills/ automatically.

Changes:
- Move skills/ and agents/ into plugins/agents-initializer/
- Move .claude-plugin/plugin.json into plugins/agents-initializer/.claude-plugin/
- Fix marketplace.json: source './plugins/agents-initializer', add $schema,
  version, description; remove invalid metadata wrapper
- Simplify plugin.json: remove fields not in official spec
- Update README: manual install paths, repository structure diagram,
  add note explaining why plugins/ subdirectory is used

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: strengthen skills based on RED-GREEN-REFACTOR testing

- init-claude: add dual-category rules guidance (convention + domain-critical)
- init-claude: add library/shared package scope detection guidance
- improve-claude: enforce 15-40 line root target explicitly
- improve-claude: add library package scope detection + domain-critical rules
- scope-detector: add criteria for shared packages and security-sensitive data

Testing results (against political-authority-highlighter):
- RED baseline: 131-line root, no .claude/rules/, dir listings in 4/6 files
- GREEN with skills: 22-line root, 2 rules files, 0 dir listings
- VERIFY after refactor: 33-line root, 5 rules files, packages/db+shared covered

commit.message:
fix(theme): fix dynamic border opacity reactivity and App.vue import

- Introduce --border-hsl and --input-hsl intermediary vars in :root and .dark
- Define --border and --input only in :root using var(--border-hsl) so CSS
  lazy evaluation keeps opacity reactive when --ui-border-opacity changes via JS
- Remove --border and --input from .dark (they cascade with correct dark base)
- Fix App.vue: import use3dfConfig from @3df-spa/ui (not @3df/ui) to share
  the correct singleton config ref with all 3df components

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: 3df-ui
repository.description: Este es un ui components de 3df SpA creado por eternalChronos

commit.message:
fix(theme): replace color-mix with hsl alpha for border opacity

color-mix(gray, transparent) darkens color toward black, making borders
invisible on dark backgrounds. Replace with hsl(h s l / opacity) which
correctly controls only the alpha channel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Raise --ui-border-opacity default: 0.2 -> 0.5 in theme.css
- Add border-color: var(--border) to @utility border-ui (self-contained)
- Update DEFAULT_CONFIG.borderOpacity: 0.2 -> 0.5 in use-3df-config.ts
- Refactor borderOpacity to 0-1 decimal range (consistent with CSS)

Fixes popover, dropdown, dialog, tooltip and all overlay borders

commit.message:
fix: improve live market outcome detection and filtering

Changes:
- Filter souvenirs from inputs (they can NEVER be trade-up inputs)
- Use live market data for outcome detection (more reliable than static DB)
- Fall back to static DB if needed
- Fix outcome price fetching using actual listing prices
- Improved outcome price discovery: prioritize live listings over API

Testing:
- Mock mode: 3 trade-ups found with correct outcomes (46-47% ROI)
- Live mode: Market data processed correctly, filters applied
- Outcomes correctly detected from live data
- 145 tradable inputs + 75 unique outputs identified

Next steps:
- Live market currently has no profitable trade-ups (price volatility)
- System is working correctly and reliably
- Ready for continuous monitoring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use static collection database for reliable trade-up outcomes

- Created collections.py with static Collection -> Rarity -> Items mapping
- Replaces unreliable API-based outcome discovery
- Ensures correct outcomes regardless of current market listings
- Fixes souvenir detection issues
- Mock mode now produces reliable, predictable results

Trade-up outcomes are now ALWAYS correct:
- AK-47 | Redline (R2) → 2 outputs (50% each)
- Glock-18 | Fade (R2) → 2 outputs (50% each)
- MP9 | Hot Rod (R1) → 2 outputs (50% each)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- core/: logger, config_loader, tool_registry, task_runner, permission,

- configs/config.toml, requirements.txt, pyproject.toml

- Gate rewards changed to soldiers-only (no more weapon upgrades)

Implement 10 major gameplay system changes

1. Hard collision lock - army can't cross center wall dividers
2. Difficulty system - 2-3x enemy counts, within-segment HP scaling, higher boss HP
3. Negative choices - segments 3/4/7 have negative safe rewards forcing risk decisions
4. Weapon barrel system - collectible weapon barrels (assault/shotgun/minigun/rocket)
5. Map variation system - 5 layout patterns randomized per run
6. Army size control - soft cap 50, hard cap 100, diminishing returns
7. Companion combat fix - dragons/turrets/drones deal actual damage
8. Game variety - 6 extra segment types mixed into cycles 2+
9. Enemy variety - 4 new types (shield/jumping/ranged/charger)
10. Balance tuning - scroll speed 15, start 3 soldiers, medic 8s interval

fix: improve scan pipeline and trade-lock detection

- Use scan_full_market() to fetch both input and output listings

- Improve verbose output with correct total count

fix: correct LegacyThoughtV0 binary layout to match actual 0.5.1 Thought struct

The migration struct was invented with phantom agent_name/agent_owner
fields that never existed in the binary Thought layout, and was missing
schema_version, signing_key_id, and thought_signature — causing a
mis-aligned decoder that failed with:

  UUID parsing failed: invalid length: expected 16 bytes, found 1

Fix: LegacyThoughtV0 now exactly mirrors the 0.5.1 Thought binary
layout, with Vec<LegacyThoughtRelation> (2-field) for relations.

Also fix the test fixture LegacyThoughtV0Record to match.
migrate_legacy_thoughts no longer references removed agent_name/owner.

145 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: move Reframe to end of ThoughtType enum to preserve bincode variant indices

Inserting Reframe before Surprise shifted Surprise from index 27→28,
corrupting all existing persisted Surprise thoughts and breaking chain
migration with 'UUID parsing failed: invalid length: expected 16 bytes,
found 1'.

New order: ...Summary(26), Surprise(27), Reframe(28)

Added maintainer note to the enum warning against mid-enum insertions.

Rule: new ThoughtType variants MUST be appended at the END. Bincode
encodes enum variants by integer index — inserting mid-enum silently
corrupts all persisted data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Support dashboard resources auto-downloading (issue #985) (#1062)

* Initial plan

* feat: add dashboard auto-download support (external-ui-url config)

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: IPv6 literals fail in direct outbound when dns.ipv6 is disabled (#1063)

* fix(tun): use async sleep to wait for TUN interface visibility before route setup (#1060)

* Initial plan

* fix(tun): wait for tun visibility using async sleep before adding routes

Fixes #1032 - intermittent TUN startup crash "tun interface not found".

After build_async() creates a new TUN device, the interface may not be
immediately visible via NetworkInterface::show(). This caused maybe_add_routes()
to call get_interface_by_name() -> None -> .expect() -> panic (SIGABRT).

Changes:
- Add Clone derive to TunConfig (config/internal/config.rs)
- Replace .expect("tun interface not found") with proper error return in
  maybe_add_routes (routes/mod.rs)
- Make new_internal an async associated function taking &TunConfig; add
  retry loop (up to 40x50ms = 2s) using tokio::time::sleep (not
  std::thread::sleep which blocks Tokio runtime) to wait for TUN
  interface visibility before calling maybe_add_routes (runner.rs)
- Update run_async to clone cfg and await new_internal inside tokio::spawn

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): address review comments - fix poll loop and surface NetworkInterface errors

- Restructure TUN visibility poll loop to avoid sleeping after the
  last attempt, eliminating false-negative window where interface
  becomes visible during the final sleep but still errors
- Capture NetworkInterface::show() errors and include the last error
  message in the Error::Operation return for better diagnostics
- Fix fmt/clippy issues in runner.rs

commit.message:
fix: move Danish shortText from shortTextEn to shortTextDa

'Ledig stilling ved X' is always Danish boilerplate. 74 job postings
had this text incorrectly placed in shortTextEn — moved to shortTextDa
and set shortTextEn to null. Applied to both job-posting and
recruitment-process files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve language detection using title field as primary indicator

25 more postings corrected where English text had Danish footer boilerplate,
causing the character-ratio heuristic to incorrectly classify as Danish.
Now uses titleEn/titleDa as authoritative language signal with text analysis
as fallback. Result: 83 Danish, 83 English postings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mentisdb
repository.description: Memory that lasts and compounds. MentisDB gives agents durable memory so they do not just remember, they improve over time. It stores append-only thought chains   plus a Git-like skills registry, letting skills evolve with experience through versioned, integrity-checked updates and fast retrieval.

commit.message:
Use SVG for socials instead of cesis fonts (#152)

* Replace Cesis font icons with embedded SVG social icons

- Create social-icons.html macro with embedded SVG paths for all 5 social platforms
- Update header and footer templates to use the new SVG-based social icons macro
- Add CSS rules for SVG icon styling with proper sizing and color inheritance
- Icons now render as scalable vector graphics instead of icon font characters
- Maintains existing styling and hover behaviors with smooth transitions

This removes dependency on the Cesis icon font for social icons, improving performance
and maintainability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update social icons with official Simple Icons CDN SVGs

- Replace placeholder SVG paths with actual paths from Simple Icons CDN
- Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp, TripAdvisor, and YouTube icons now use official designs
- Updated fill attribute to 'currentColor' for proper color inheritance
- Icons maintain consistent styling and size across all pages

Fetched from:
- https://cdn.simpleicons.org/instagram
- https://cdn.simpleicons.org/facebook
- https://cdn.simpleicons.org/whatsapp
- https://cdn.simpleicons.org/tripadvisor
- https://cdn.simpleicons.org/youtube

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused Cesis icon font and legacy WordPress theme files

- Delete entire static/wordpress directory containing Cesis theme
- Remove @font-face definition for tticons icon font
- Remove unused .fa and .fa-* CSS rules
- Update CSS load order documentation
- Cesis files no longer needed after SVG social icons implementation

This reduces codebase size and removes legacy WordPress theme dependencies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update Instagram SVG and clean up fill attributes

- Remove fill="currentColor" from SVG elements in macro
- Add fill: currentColor and path { fill: inherit } to CSS for proper color inheritance
- Instagram SVG now matches official Simple Icons format without inline fill

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix instagram

* Delete .claude/settings.local.json

* Delete package-lock.json

* Update templates/_macros/social-icons.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add rel="noopener noreferrer" to external social media links

- Add security-critical rel attribute to Instagram, Facebook, TripAdvisor, and YouTube links
- Prevents reverse-tabnabbing and protects window.opener from external sites
- WhatsApp link is internal (/whatsapp/) and doesn't require this attribute

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused css

* Revert "Remove unused css"

This reverts commit be2cc9418e80278f0c7f0908e8d3809111ffb425.

* Replace tticons font-family with Unicode checkmark in checkbox styling

Remove the font-family: "tticons" line and replace content: "\f00c" with content: "✓" in the checkbox :checked:before selector. This preserves all other checkbox styling while removing the dependency on the legacy tticons icon font.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

updated titleEn/shortTextEn and set languages=[en]. List endpoint synced.
